text: 1) Physics – Multiple Choice Which of the following is TRUE of the wave model of atomic theory? W) Electrons orbit the nucleus in circles X) Electrons have a well-defined position and momentum at all points in their orbit Y) Electrons compose most of the mass of the atom Z) Electrons can absorb photons to transition between states
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Z) ELECTRONS CAN ABSORB PHOTONS TO TRANSITION BETWEEN STATES
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER

text: 1) Physics – Multiple Choice When honey is poured from a jar, the flowing honey column gets narrower and narrower as it falls. Which of the following best explains the observed profile for steady flow? W) Flow rate decreases but speed remains constant with distance X) Flow rate remains constant but speed increases with distance Y) Viscosity increases with distance from the jar Z) Turbulence increases with distance from the jar
label: BONUS_TEXT

text: ANSWER: X) FLOW RATE REMAINS CONSTANT BUT SPEED INCREASES WITH DISTANCE
label: BONUS_ANSWER

text: 7) Physics – Multiple Choice A cue ball moving due northeast strikes a stationary billiard ball of equal mass. The other ball begins moving due east. What can be said about the final direction of the cue ball? W) It moves due west X) It moves due north Y) It moves due south Z) It continues moving due northeast
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: X) IT MOVES DUE NORTH

text: 13) Physics – Short Answer In a system consisting of two bodies acting on each other via a central force, the angular momentum of the system is constant and dependent on the reduced mass of the system, the angular velocity, and the distance squared. text: ANSWER: SECOND (ACCEPT: LAW OF EQUAL AREAS IN EQUAL TIME)

text: 20) Physics – Multiple Choice Which of the following best describes the gravitational potential for a uniform spherical body as a function of radius? W) Constant inside the body, and proportional to 1/r^2 outside the body X) Linearly increasing inside the body, and proportional to 1/r^3 outside the body Y) Quadratically increasing inside the body, and proportional to 1/r outside the body Z) Constant everywhere
label: TOSSUP_TEXT

text: ANSWER: Y) QUADRATICALLY INCREASING INSIDE THE BODY, AND PROPORTIONAL TO 1/R OUTSIDE THE BODY
label: TOSSUP_ANSWER
